# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.

Write secure code.
# Category: container_security

# Start from a python-2.7 based ubuntu environment as it's more compatible with existing system setup, including the use of apt packages for installing new libraries etc..
FROM python:2.7-slim  # Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies  
WORKDIR /app   
# Copy local code to container image work directory    
COPY . ./     
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt      
EXPOSE $PORT          # Expose port for the application, so it can be accessed outside of Docker 
CMD ["python", "manage.py","runserver"]
